Criterion: Analysis: Identification, description, and support for the hypothesis
Excellent - Very Good: A reasonable hypothesis for the observed trends and evidence in support of claim.
Good: A reasonable hypothesis for the observed trends and evidence in support of claim.
Satisfactory: A basic identification and explanation of course content in support of the main claim/warrant.
Unsatisfactory: A partial or incorrect identification and explanation of course content in support of the main claim/warrant

Excellent - Very Good: all graphs correctly depicting the data, with properly labeled axes and a title
Good: most graphs correctly depicting the data, with most axes properly labeled and a title
Satisfactory: several errors in graphs, multiple errors in the labeling of axes, titles missing
Unsatisfactory: many errors in graphs or graphs missing, multiple errors in the labeling of axes, most titles missing

Excellent - Very Good: appropriate number of citations, citations from the scholarly literature, citations properly formatted
Good: appropriate number of citations, citations from the scholarly literature, citations properly formatted
Satisfactory: fewer citations than required or fewer from scholarly literature than expected; some errors in formatting
Unsatisfactory: no or only 1 citation, not from the scholarly literature, many errors

Excellent - Very Good: The paper is easy to follow, it is well organized, and the reader can understand the individual sentences and paragraphs without having to re-read them.
Good: The paper is for the most part understandable and well organized, but a couple of sentences are confusing and difficult for the reader to understand.
Satisfactory: The paper is understandable, but there are some points when it is difficult to follow the author's argument.
Unsatisfactory: The paper is difficult to follow, the argument is disorganized and confusing, and many sentences are difficult to understand.

Excellent - Very Good: Without any grammatical, syntactical, or spelling errors.
Good: With only one or two spelling or grammatical errors
Satisfactory: Only a few minor grammatical and spelling errors.
Unsatisfactory: Either many minor errors or a few serious errors
